title: Research
text:
Job Market Paper (in progress, 2026)
I study how improvements in domestic market access affect non-cocoa, non-mining forest loss in Ghana between 2001 and 2021. I build a structural trade model linking district-level prices to a market-access index, and identify causal effects using a recentered instrumental variable that exploits plausibly exogenous variation in transport-cost shocks.
Methods: structural trade model, panel IV, recentered instruments, Google Earth Engine, high-resolution land-cover data.
Draft (coming soon) · Slides · Code

title: Research

title: Abdul Baari Bakpa
text:
I am a graduate student in economics at Sciences Po, Paris. My research sits at the intersection of development economics, trade, and environmental economics, with a focus on Sub-Saharan Africa.
My current work studies how market access shapes deforestation in Ghana, combining a structural trade model with high-resolution satellite data and a panel instrumental-variable strategy.
